sections.paragraph:
rarely חֵק Pro 17:23, with suff. חֵיקִי Psa 35:13, and חֵקִי Job 19:27, m. [“the bosom, i.e. the breast with the arms, so called from embracing, see the root חוּק ”].

(1) bosom of a garment, Pro 16:33 שֹׁחַד בַּחֵק “a present (given) into the bosom,” i.e. given secretly, Pro 21:14 comp. Pro 17:23. (Lat. sinum laxare, expedire, used of an expectant of gifts, see Senec. Epist. 119. Thyest. 430.)

(2) the bosom of a person. שָׁכַב בְּחֵיק to lie in the bosom (of a woman) de complexu venereo. The phrase שָׁכַב בְּחֵיק is “to lie in a consort’s bosom,” 1Ki 1:2 Mic 7:5 a mother’s, 1Ki 3:20 (of an infant, comp. Rth 4:16). Hence it is applied to intimate conjugal love, אֵשֶׁת חֵיקֶךָ the wife who is in thy bosom, Deu 13:7 28:54 compare verse 56 Deu 28:56. שִׁלֵּם אֶל חֵיק Jer 32:18 הֵשִׁיב אֶל חֵיק פּ׳ Psa 79:12, to recompense to any one into the bosom (as God the actions of men), i.q. elsewhere חֵשִׁיב בְּרֹאשׁ Jud. 9:57 1Sa 25:39 Joel 4:7 . (Winer is altogether wrong in taking this expression to signify full measure (Lex. p. 323) to be received not by the hand but into the bosom of a garment, compare Luk 6:38 the phrase simply means that something is made to return from whence it came; compare the similar Arabic expression رد ىذ خحوره to return upon one’s neck, Hist. Tim. tom. i. p. 30, Mang.) It is spoken of the breast for the mind or soul, Job 19:27 [?]; Ecc 7:9. [“Also i.q. קֶרֶב Job 19:27.”]

(3) Metaph. the bosom of a chariot, i.e. its hollow part, 1Ki 22:35 the bosom of the altar, the lower or hollowed part for the fire, in which it is kept burning, Eze 43:13.
